arts.ttl: Leeds United have already pulled off transfer masterstroke ahead of summer window
arts.sum: Sky Sports pundit Don Goodman told Football League World: “I think if you’re a Championship club, and at the moment Leeds still are, and you can get £7.7 million for a player that hasn’t been a part of your plans and who’s not likely to be part of your plans then it’s a great deal. High-flying Bundesliga club Eintracht Frankfurt, who were dumped out of the Europa League by Tottenham Hotspur on Thursday evening, have signed Kristensen on a permanent basis for a fee thought to be in the region of £7.7m .

arts.ttl: Stoke City badge change: next stage and design process explained
arts.sum: More than 5,000 Stoke City supporters have joined in consultation about potential changes to the club badge – and given a clear steer about what they want and don’t want in any new design. A total 81 per cent of fans who took part in initial surveys said they were open to the idea of a new badge in the first major review of the crest that was introduced back in 2001.

arts.ttl: Leeds United: 3 free agents 49ers must consider
arts.sum: Leeds United have three games left in the Championship with automatic promotion in their hands, and with that there is speculation around how the club might tackle the upcoming summer window. Atalanta midfielder Mario Pasalic’s contract is due to expire in the summer at the age of 30, and even though his previous stint in England was a failure at Chelsea, not playing a single league game, he’s gone from strength to strength in Italy.

arts.ttl: Leeds ready to replace Illan Meslier with international goalkeeper
arts.sum: Illan Meslier has had another tough season in the Leeds goal, and because of this, it is not surprise that he could be replaced during the summer transfer window. He has been dropped in recent weeks by head coach Daniel Farke, but he could lose further prominence ahead of the 2025-26 campaign, and a move […] The post Leeds ready to replace Illan Meslier with international goalkeeper appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Loan rules are a nonsense for clubs like Stoke City
arts.sum: On a tangent, Nathan Lowe was actually having a storming first loan out at Walsall to the extent that he's up for League Two player of the season despite spending the second half of it largely kicking his heels at Stoke. It was bizarre when it suddenly became the norm to play 10 or 15 minutes added time each game a year-and-a-half ago but you could understand the point about wanting the ball to be in play more and this was a prod in that direction.

arts.ttl: Potential Leeds United XI vs Stoke as Daniel Farke faces Daniel James conundrum
arts.sum: Manor Solomon came off due to “cramps” in the Oxford win but Farke believes the in-form attacker will be fine to start against Stoke. Predicted XI: Darlow; Bogle, Rodon, Ampadu, Firpo; Tanaka, Gruev; Gnonto, Aaronson, Solomon; Piroe.

arts.ttl: How Leeds United fans can watch crucial Burnley vs Sheffield United promotion clash for free
arts.sum: Leeds United fans will be able to watch Burnley vs Sheffield United for free on Easter Monday - and that could be the day Daniel Farke's side secure promotion if all goes to plan. As the Whites had an ideal start to the Easter weekend, with a 1-0 win over Oxford United on Good Friday, it could end perfectly on Easter Monday with the club having a chance to secure promotion that day if results go their way.
